title: Object
subtitle: The root proto. All other protos clone from Object; it provides core slot management, message dispatch, introspection, and control flow primitives.

members.description: The '' method evaluates the argument and returns the result.
Returns true the receiver is not equal to aValue, false otherwise.

Returns the negative version of aNumber.
    Raises an exception if argument is not a number.
.. is an alias for: method(arg, self asString append(arg asString))
Evaluates argument and returns self if self is less or Nil if not.

Evaluates argument and returns self if self is less
    than or equal to it, or Nil if not.
Returns true if receiver and aValue are equal, false otherwise.

Evaluates argument and returns self if self is greater than it, or Nil if
    not.

Evaluates argument and returns self if self is greater
    than or equal to it, or Nil if not.

description: Sends the message aMessage to the receiver if it can respond to it. Example: 
	MyObject test // performs test
	MyObject ?test // performs test if MyObject has a slot named test
The search for the slot only follows the receivers proto chain.

Sends asynchronous message to an object, returns a FutureProxy.
Caller coroutine is paused when proxy is accessed (i.e. message is sent) till result is ready. Proxy will become an actual result when it is ready.
See IoGuide for more information.
Usage: obj @someMethod(a, b, c)

Same as Object @, but returns nil instead of FutureProxy.
Might be useful in a command line or as a last expression in a block/method when you don't want to return a future.

members.name: actorProcessQueue
members.description: Processes each message in a queue, yielding between each message.

members.name: actorRun
members.description: Starts actor mode if not started already. Basically, sets actorProcessQueue for later execution.

members.name: addTrait
members.description: Takes another object, whose slots will be copied into the receiver. Optionally takes a second argument, a Map object containing string -> string pairs, holding conflicting slot names and names to rename them to. I.e., if you have two objects A and B, both have a slot named foo, you issue A addTrait(B, Map clone atPut("foo", "newFoo")) the value of B foo will be placed in A newFoo.

members.name: ancestorWithSlot
members.description:
Returns the first ancestor of the receiver that contains
    a slot of the specified name or Nil if none is found.

members.name: ancestors
members.description: Returns a list of all of the receiver's ancestors as found by recursively following the protos links.

members.name: and
members.description: Evaluates argument and returns the result.

members.name: appendProto
members.description: Appends anObject to the receiver's proto list. Returns self.

members.name: apropos
members.description: Prints out <tt>Protos Core</tt> slot descriptions.

members.name: argIsActivationRecord
members.description: Note: seems to be an obsolete method.

members.name: argIsCall
members.description:
Returns true if arg is an activation context (i.e. Call object)
Note: this is used internally in one place only (Coroutine callStack). Refactoring should be considered.

members.name: asSimpleString
members.description: Returns <type>_<uniqueHexId> string.

members.name: asString
members.description: Same as <tt>slotSummary</tt>.

members.name: become
members.description:
Replaces receiver with anotherObject and returns self. Useful for implementing transparent proxies. See also FutureProxy and Object @.
Note: primitives cannot become new values.

members.name: block
members.description:
Creates a block and binds it to the sender context (i.e. lexical context). In other words, block locals' proto is sender's locals. args is a list of formal arguments (can be empty).
body is evaluated in the context of Locals object.
See also Object method. 
          Io> block(x, x*2) scope == thisContext
    ==> true

members.name: break
members.description: Break the current loop, if any.

members.name: callcc
checkMemory
members.description:
Accesses memory in the IoObjectData struct that should be accessible.
   Should cause a memory access exception if memory is corrupt.

members.name: clone
members.description: Returns a clone of the receiver.

members.name: cloneWithoutInit
members.description: Returns a clone of the receiver but does not call init.

members.name: compact
members.description: Compact the memory for the object if possible. Returns self.

members.name: compactState
members.description: Attempt to compact the memory of the IoState if possible.

members.name: compare
members.description:
Returns a number containing the comparison value of the target with
    anObject.

members.name: contextWithSlot
members.description:
Returns the first context (starting with the receiver and following the
    lookup path) that contains a slot of the specified name or Nil if none is
    found.

members.name: continue
members.description:
Skip the rest of the current loop iteration and start on
    the next, if any.

members.name: coroDo
members.description:
Creates a new coro to be run in a context of sender and yields to it.
		Returns a coro.

members.name: coroDoLater
members.description:
Returns a new coro to be run in a context of sender. New coro is moved to the top of the yieldingCoros queue to be executed when current coro yields.
Note: run target is self (i.e. receiver), not call sender as in coroDo.

members.name: coroFor
members.description: Returns a new coro to be run in a context of sender.

members.name: coroWith
members.description: Returns a new coro to be run in a context of receiver.

members.name: currentCoro
members.description: Returns the currently running coroutine.

members.name: deprecatedWarning
members.description:
Prints a warning message that the current method is deprecated.
	If optionalNewName is supplied, the warning will suggest using that instead.
	Returns self.

members.name: do
members.description: Evaluates the message in the context of the receiver. Returns self.

members.name: doFile
members.description:
Evaluates the File in the context of the receiver. Returns the result.
    pathString is relative to the current working directory.

members.name: doMessage
members.description:
Evaluates the message object in the context of the receiver.
    Returns the result. optionalContext can be used to specific the locals
    context in which the message is evaluated.

members.name: doRelativeFile
members.description:
Evaluates the File in the context of the receiver. Returns the result. 
		pathString is relative to the file calling doRelativeFile. (Duplicate of relativeDoFile)

members.name: doString
members.description: Evaluates the string in the context of the receiver. Returns the result.

members.name: evalArg
members.description: The '' method evaluates the argument and returns the result.

members.name: evalArgAndReturnNil
members.description: Evaluates the argument and returns nil.

members.name: evalArgAndReturnSelf
members.description: Evaluates the argument and returns the target.

members.name: for
members.description:
A for-loop control structure. See the io Programming Guide for a full
    description.

members.name: foreach
members.description:
For each slot, set name to the slot's name and value to the slot's value and execute message. Examples: 

    myObject foreach(n, v,
            writeln("slot ", n, " = ", v type)
    )

    myObject foreach(v,
            writeln("slot type ", v type)
    )

members.name: foreachSlot
members.description:
Iterates over all the slots in a receiver. Provides slotValue (non-activated) along with slotName. Code is executed in context of sender. slotName and slotValue become visible in the receiver (no Locals created! Maybe, it is not the best decision). 
  Io> thisContext foreachSlot(n, v, n println)
  Lobby
  Protos
  exit
  forward
  n
  v
  ==> false

members.name: forward
members.description:
Called when the receiver is sent a message it doesn't recognize. Default implementation raises an "Object doesNotRespond" exception. Subclasses can override this method to implement proxies or special error handling.

Example: 

 myProxy forward = method( messageName :=
thisMessage name arguments := thisMessage arguments myObject
doMessage(thisMessage)
)

members.name: getLocalSlot
members.description:
Returns the value of the slot named slotNameString
    (not looking in the object's protos) or nil if no such slot is found.

members.name: getSlot
members.description:
Returns the value of the slot named slotNameString
    (following the lookup path) or nil if no such slot is found.

members.name: handleActorException
members.description:
Callback for handling exceptions during asynchronous message processing.
Default value: method(e, e showStack)

members.name: hasLocalSlot
members.description: Returns true if the slot exists in the receiver or false otherwise.

members.name: hasProto
members.description:
Returns true if anObject is found in the proto path of the target, false
    otherwise.

members.name: hasSlot
members.description:
Returns <tt>true</tt> if slot is found somewhere in the inheritance chain 
  (including receiver itself).

members.name: if
members.description:
Evaluates trueMessage if condition evaluates to a non-Nil.
    Otherwise evaluates optionalFalseMessage if it is present.
    Returns the result of the evaluated message or Nil if none was evaluated.

members.name: ifError
members.description: Does nothing, returns self.

members.name: ifNil
members.description: Does nothing, returns self.

members.name: ifNilEval
members.description: Does nothing, returns self.

members.name: ifNonNil
members.description: Evaluates argument and returns self.

members.name: ifNonNilEval
members.description: Evaluates argument and returns the result.

members.name: in
members.description: Same as: aList contains(self)

members.name: inlineMethod
members.description:
Creates a method which is executed directly in a receiver (no Locals object is created). 
  Io> m := inlineMethod(x := x*2)
  Io> x := 1
  ==> 1
  Io> m
  ==> 2
  Io> m
  ==> 4
  Io> m
  ==> 8

members.name: isActivatable
members.description: Returns true if the receiver is activatable, false otherwise.

members.name: isError
members.description: Returns false if not an error.

members.name: isIdenticalTo
members.description: Returns true if the receiver is identical to aValue, false otherwise.

members.name: isKindOf
members.description: Returns true if anObject is in the receiver's ancestors.

members.name: isLaunchScript
members.description: Returns true if the current file was run on the command line. Io's version of Python's __file__ == "__main__"

members.name: isNil
members.description: Returns false.

members.name: isTrue
members.description: Returns true.

members.name: justSerialized
members.description:
Writes serialized representation to a SerializationStream. Returns stream contents. 
  [This is unintended side effect! Returned value may change in the future.]

members.name: launchFile
members.description: Eval file at pathString as if from the command line in its folder.

members.name: lazySlot
members.description:
Defines a slot with a lazy initialization code. Code is run only once: the first time slot is accessed. Returned value is stored in a regular slot. 
  Io> x := lazySlot("Evaluated!" println; 17)
  Io> x
  Evaluated!
  ==> 17
  Io> x
  ==> 17
  Io> x
  ==> 17

Another form is lazySlot(name, code): 
  Io> lazySlot("x", "Evaluated!" println; 17)
  Io> x
  Evaluated!
  ==> 17
  Io> x
  ==> 17
  Io> x
  ==> 17

members.name: lexicalDo
members.description:
Evaluates the message in the context of the receiver.
    The lexical context is added as a proto of the receiver while the argument
    is evaluated. Returns self.

members.name: list
members.description: Returns a List containing the arguments.

members.name: localsForward
members.description: CFunction used by Locals prototype for forwarding.

members.name: localsUpdateSlot
members.description: Local's version of updateSlot mthod.

members.name: loop
members.description: Keeps evaluating message until a break.

members.name: markClean
members.description: Cleans object's slots.

members.name: memorySize
members.description: Return the amount of memory used by the object.

members.name: memorySizeOfState
members.description:
Returns the number of bytes in the IoState
    (this may not include memory allocated by C libraries).

members.name: message
members.description:
Return the message object for the argument or Nil if there is no argument.
    Note: returned object is a mutable singleton. Use "message(foo) clone" if
you wish to modify it.

members.name: method
members.description:
Creates a method. args is a list of formal arguments (can be empty).
body is evaluated in the context of Locals object.
Locals' proto is a message receiver (i.e. self).
Slot with a method is activatable. Use getSlot(name) to retrieve method object without activating it (i.e. calling).
See also Object block.

members.name: newSlot
members.description:
Creates a getter and setter for the slot with the name slotName
	and sets its default value to aValue. Returns self. For example,
	newSlot("foo", 1) would create slot named foo with the value 1 as well as a setter method setFoo().

members.name: not
members.description: Returns nil.

members.name: or
members.description: Returns true.

members.name: ownsSlots
members.description: A debug method.

members.name: pause
members.description:
Removes current coroutine from the yieldingCoros queue and yields to another coro. Exits if no coros left.
See Coroutine documentation for more details.

members.name: perform
members.description: Performs the method corresponding to methodName with the arguments supplied.

members.name: performWithArgList
members.description:
Performs the method corresponding to methodName with the arguments in the
    argList.

members.name: prependProto
members.description: Prepends anObject to the receiver's proto list. Returns self.

members.name: print
members.description: Prints a string representation of the object. Returns Nil.

members.name: println
members.description: Same as print, but also prints a new line. Returns self.

members.name: proto
members.description: Same as; method(self protos first)

members.name: protos
members.description: Returns a copy of the receiver's protos list.

members.name: raiseIfError
members.description: Does nothing, returns self.

members.name: relativeDoFile
members.description:
Evaluates the File in the context of the receiver. Returns the result. 
		pathString is relative to the file calling doRelativeFile. (Duplicate of doRelativeFile)

members.name: removeAllProtos
members.description: Removes all of the receiver's protos. Returns self.

members.name: removeAllSlots
members.description: Removes all of the receiver's slots. Returns self.

members.name: removeProto
members.description:
Removes anObject from the receiver's proto list if it
    is present. Returns self.

members.name: removeSlot
members.description:
Removes the specified slot (only) in the receiver if it exists. Returns
    self.

members.name: resend
members.description:
Send the message used to activate the current method to the Object's proto. For example: 
  Dog := Mammal clone do(
    init := method(
  	  resend
    )
  )
Calling Dog init will send an init method to Mammal, but using the Dog's context.

members.name: return
members.description: Return anObject from the current execution block.

members.name: returnIfError
members.description: Does nothing, returns self.

members.name: returnIfNonNil
members.description:
Returns the receiver from the current execution block if it is non nil.
    Otherwise returns the receiver locally.

members.name: self
members.description: Returns self.

members.name: serialized
members.description:
Returns a serialized representation of the receiver. 
  Io> Object clone do(x:=1) serialized
  ==> Object clone do(
  	x := 1
  )

members.name: serializedSlots
members.description: Writes all slots to a stream.

members.name: serializedSlotsWithNames
members.description: Writes selected slots to a stream.

members.name: setIsActivatable
members.description:
When called with a non-Nil aValue, sets the object
    to call its activate slot when accessed as a value. Turns this behavior
    off if aValue is Nil. Only works on Objects which are not Activatable
    Primitives (such as CFunction or Block). Returns self.

members.name: setProto
members.description:
Sets the first proto of the receiver to anObject, replacing the
    current one, if any. Returns self.

members.name: setProtos
members.description: Replaces the receiver's protos with a copy of aList. Returns self.

members.name: setSlot
members.description:
Sets the slot slotNameString in the receiver to
    hold valueObject. Returns valueObject.

members.name: setSlotWithType
members.description:
Sets the slot slotNameString in the receiver to
    hold valueObject and sets the type slot of valueObject
    to be slotNameString. Returns valueObject.

members.name: shallowCopy
members.description: Returns a shallow copy of the receiver.

members.name: slotDescriptionMap
members.description:
Returns raw map of slot names and short values' descriptions.
  See also <tt>Object slotSummary</tt>.

members.name: slotNames
members.description:
Returns a list of strings containing the names of the
    slots in the receiver (but not in its lookup path).

members.name: slotSummary
members.description:
Returns a formatted slotDescriptionMap. 
  Io> slotSummary
  ==>  Object_0x30c590:
    Lobby            = Object_0x30c590
    Protos           = Object_0x30c880
    exit             = method(...)
    forward          = method(...)

members.name: slotValues
members.description: Returns a list of the values held in the slots of the receiver.

members.name: stopStatus
members.description: Returns the internal IoState->stopStatus.

members.name: super
members.description:
Sends the message aMessage to the receiver's proto with the context of self. Example: 
	self test(1, 2)   // performs test(1, 2) on self
	super(test(1, 2)) // performs test(1, 2) on self proto but with the context of self

members.name: switch
members.description: Execute an expression depending on the value of the caller. (This is an equivalent to C switch/case) hour := Date hour switch( 12, "midday", 0, "midnight", 17, "teatime", Date hour asString )

members.name: thisContext
members.description: Synonym to self.

members.name: thisLocalContext
members.description: Returns current locals.

members.name: thisMessage
members.description: Returns the calling message (i.e. thisMessage itself, huh).

members.name: try
members.description:
Executes particular code in a new coroutine. Returns exception or nil if no exception is caught.
See also documentation for Exception catch and pass.

members.name: type
members.description:
Returns a string containing the name of the type of Object (Number, String,
    etc).

members.name: uniqueHexId
members.description:
Returns uniqueId in a hexadecimal form (with a "0x" prefix) 
  Io> Object uniqueId
  ==> 3146784
  Io> Object uniqueHexId
  ==> 0x300420

members.name: uniqueId
members.description: Returns a Number containing a unique id for the receiver.

members.name: updateSlot
members.description:
Same as setSlot(), but raises an error if the slot does not
    already exist in the receiver's slot lookup path.

members.name: wait
members.description:
Pauses current coroutine for at least s seconds.
Note: current coroutine may wait much longer than designated number of seconds depending on circumstances.

members.name: while
members.description:
Keeps evaluating message until condition return Nil.
    Returns the result of the last message evaluated or Nil if none were
    evaluated.

members.name: withHandler
members.description:
Installs a resumable exception handler for exceptions matching exceptionProto,
		runs body, then removes the handler. The handlerBlock receives (exception, resume).
		To resume at the signal site, call resume invoke(value) or simply return a value.

members.name: write
members.description: Sends a print message to the evaluated result of each argument. Returns Nil.

members.name: writeln
members.description: Same as write() but also writes a return character at the end. Returns Nil.

members.name: yield
members.description:
Yields to another coroutine. Does nothing if yieldingCoros queue is empty.
See Coroutine documentation for more details.
